text: And arguably most importantly from a US context, the Miracle on Ice was extremely unlikely, and our most popular league has a draft and salary cap to ensure parity, plus it plays just a 17 game season, and its founding mythos is that "on any given Sunday" any team in the league could beat any other team. Arguably our second most popular sporting event is the NCAA tournament, a 6 round, single elimination bracket designed to produce high-variance outcomes, with a major focus of media attention being on the "cinderella" teams that pull off multiple upsets. One of the most founding principles of American sports fandom (and arguably american culture as a whole) is that the underdogs ALWAYS have a shot. I'd expect that any team in the top ~15-20 has some level of belief that they can win the World Cup, you have to have that belief to get to that level, but I'm not at all surprised that the US team believes it's possible.
label: Evidence-Based Take
